question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the service with maximum-gas requests (params.getGas() at ceiling) to fully drain the ThrottleManager gas bucket, causing all subsequent block-processing calls to stall or be rejected for a duration equivalent to 500%+ of average block time, effectively freezing transaction throughput?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: restoreGasToBucket()] Can an unprivileged user craft requests that always result in result==null (by triggering an exception path before result is assigned) so that restoreGasToBucket() always restores only gasLimitRefundPercent of gasLimit rather than the actual remaining gas, causing the throttle bucket to deplete faster than it refills and delaying block processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user submit a request with a BlockType that resolves to a non-existent block, causing the memoized blockSupplier (via Suppliers.memoize()) to throw BlockNumberNotFoundException on every access within the ContractCallContext, stalling all concurrent block lookups and delaying the effective block advancement by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Suppliers.memoize() call on blockSupplier to cache a stale or far-future block reference within a ContractCallContext, causing subsequent operations within the same context to operate on a frozen block state and delaying effective block progression by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user repeatedly submit ETH_ESTIMATE_GAS calls (isEstimate=true) which bypass the restoreGasToBucket() call in the finally block, causing the gas throttle bucket to be consumed without restoration and blocking all real block-processing transactions for a period exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the MirrorEvmTransactionException catch branch (which sets result = e.getResult()) with a null getResult() return, causing restoreGasToBucket(null, gasLimit) to always restore only the refund-percent baseline rather than remaining gas, systematically depleting the throttle bucket and freezing block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: restoreGasToBucket()] Can an unprivileged user craft a request where gasLimit is set to the maximum allowed value and the transaction always fails (result.isSuccessful()==false and gasLimit==result.gasUsed()), so that only gasLimitRefundPercent/100 * gasLimit is restored, and by repeating this pattern exhaust the throttle bucket to freeze block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getBlock()] Can an unprivileged user submit a request with a timestamp that is just above Utils.getCurrentTimestamp(), causing the filter in getBlock() to suppress the timestamp and fall back to BlockType.LATEST.toString(), masking the actual block delay and preventing operators from detecting that block processing has been delayed by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user submit concurrent requests all referencing the same far-future block number, causing recordFileService.findByBlockType() to be called repeatedly for a non-existent block, exhausting database connection pools or query threads and indirectly delaying block processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that updateMetrics() is only called when result != null and !estimate, by crafting requests that always produce a null result (via exception before assignment), to suppress all metric emission and hide a sustained block-delay attack lasting 500%+ of average block time from monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user pass BlockType.EARLIEST (block number 0) in params.getBlock() to force recordFileService.findByBlockType() to always return the genesis block, causing all EVM executions to operate on the oldest possible state and effectively freezing the visible block height at genesis for the duration of the attack, simulating a 500%+ block delay?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user send a high volume of requests with gas values just below the throttle threshold to avoid individual throttling, but collectively consume the entire gas bucket within a single block period, causing the next block's transactions to be frozen for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: restoreGasToBucket()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Math.min(gasRemaining, gasLimitToRestoreBaseline) logic in restoreGasToBucket() by submitting transactions that use almost all gas (gasRemaining near zero), ensuring minimal gas is restored to the bucket, and by repeating this pattern freeze block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the ContractCallContext.run() wrapper to create a large number of concurrent contexts, each holding a memoized blockSupplier pointing to a slow or blocking recordFileService call, causing thread pool exhaustion that delays block processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user craft a request where transactionExecutionService.execute() throws an IllegalStateException (caught and rethrown as MirrorEvmTransactionException), bypassing the result assignment, so that restoreGasToBucket(null, gasLimit) only restores the refund baseline, and by repeating this deplete the throttle bucket to freeze block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: updateMetrics()] Can an unprivileged user flood the service with requests that each emit a unique TAG_BLOCK value (by using different historical block timestamps), causing unbounded metric cardinality growth in the MeterRegistry that degrades monitoring infrastructure and masks a block-delay attack lasting 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user submit a request with a block number that is exactly one block ahead of the current chain tip, causing recordFileService.findByBlockType() to block or spin-wait for the block to appear, and by sending many such requests freeze the processing pipeline for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the estimate=true path skips both restoreGasToBucket() and updateMetrics(), allowing unlimited gas consumption without throttle restoration, and use this to freeze block processing for 500%+ of average block time without leaving any metric trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: restoreGasToBucket()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the integer arithmetic in gasLimitToRestoreBaseline = (long)(gasLimit * throttleProperties.getGasLimitRefundPercent() / 100f) by passing a gasLimit value that causes floating-point truncation to zero, resulting in zero gas being restored to the bucket and freezing block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user pass a hex-encoded block number (e.g., 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF) via BlockType.of() that parses to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing recordFileService.findByBlockType() to search for a block that will never exist, throwing BlockNumberNotFoundException and stalling the context, effectively delaying block resolution by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the sequential nature of the try-catch-finally block in doProcessCall() by crafting inputs that cause transactionExecutionService.execute() to hang indefinitely (e.g., via a contract with an infinite loop), blocking the thread and preventing block advancement for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a timeout on the Suppliers.memoize() blockSupplier to hold a ContractCallContext open indefinitely by keeping the supplier unevaluated, tying up context resources and delaying block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user send a burst of requests with the maximum allowed gas value simultaneously, overwhelming the ThrottleManager.restore() call's ability to keep up with consumption, causing the gas bucket to remain empty for a duration exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getBlock()] Can an unprivileged user craft a request with a context timestamp set to exactly Utils.getCurrentTimestamp() + 1 nanosecond, causing the filter t <= Utils.getCurrentTimestamp() to exclude it and fall back to LATEST, hiding the true block delay from metrics and allowing a 500%+ block-time delay attack to go undetected?
